label: About
title: About Remote Studio Paste-Up
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 21 recent saved-signal files and 63 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around A modern digital art studio with an artist working on a graphics tablet. The studio features a... and 0xSero - Overview, plus 7 more. A source image from canvas4everyone.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Remote Studio Paste-Up: Create one finished still image using the composition archetype of a torn poster wall and the camera plate grammar of a torn wall: a dense, front-facing paste-up surface, cropped close enough that the wall becomes the whole world. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against upper-center cyan torn aperture, middle cable seam network, and right glossy tablet-and-stylus pocket.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
